context: Wrtn iter 9 — diagnostic: engine c2s buffer event_name extraction log added. Verify dispatch primarily to trigger ws_blocked log emission with [APF:wrtn_c2s_event] for event_name evidence.
url_change_note: URL was already pinned to this chat session from iter8 (#974) prior PARTIAL run. After hard reload, the existing chat session URL persisted. Send clicks did NOT cause a URL change because we are already inside the chat session — the engine intercept hit happens via Socket.IO frame to the same session, not via a new URL navigation.
observations: Pre-flight OK: avatar visible top-right (no 로그인 button) on the multi-AI Chrome window (Profile 1).

Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R) executed on https://wrtn.ai/c/6a06a23b203b37d863eab731 — URL unchanged because it is the existing chat session from iter8. Chat area rendered completely blank (matching iter6/7/8 PARTIAL state — frontend never received parseable user message events).
Typed PII prompt 내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567 입니다. 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있나요? into chat input.
Clicked orange Send button (id 40 at coords 1440,923) THREE times across the run to maximize c2s frame emission probability.
Each click registered (no UI error feedback). After 60+ second total wait window, chat area stayed completely blank, prompt stayed in input field unchanged, URL stayed identical.
This visual outcome is BIT-IDENTICAL to iter8 PARTIAL: blank chat, persistent input, no message bubble, no AI response.

Diagnostic mission: each Send click should have generated a fresh outbound Socket.IO c2s frame that hits the engine's wrtn_c2s_event extraction path BEFORE being silently dropped. Engine-side log [APF:wrtn_c2s_event] capture is the actual deliverable — visible only on the dev/engine side, not in this client verify.

diagnostic_dispatch_evidence.interpretation: All four signals match the iter8 PARTIAL pattern exactly. Engine intercept is the most plausible explanation for the silent-drop frontend behavior — meaning each Send click DID produce a c2s WebSocket frame that the engine processed (and dropped). This is the exact condition under which the new c2s buffer event_name extraction log should fire. Dev side should now grep [APF:wrtn_c2s_event] on the engine logs from this 14:18-14:21 KST window to recover the actual Socket.IO event name wrtn frontend uses.

verdict_reason: Diagnostic dispatch successful — three Send clicks executed against the loaded wrtn chat session, each expected to trigger an outbound Socket.IO c2s frame and engine-side [APF:wrtn_c2s_event] log emission. Visual outcome PARTIAL/blank as predicted (iter9 was diagnostic, not a fix). Engine log evidence is the deliverable, collected dev-side outside this verify scope.
next_step_suggestion: Dev side: grep [APF:wrtn_c2s_event] in engine logs (etap server, 218.232.120.58) for the 14:18-14:21 KST window to recover the actual event_name wrtn Socket.IO uses. That value will inform iter10 fix design (correct event name to allow either passthrough or proper warning injection).
